'Far beyond anything we had predicted': Framework says its supplier's memory prices are 'more than double' its previous shipment
summary: The company says it "goes far beyond anything we’re able to absorb without placing our ability to operate at real financial risk."
body: Over the last few months, Framework has been doing a pretty good job of keeping RAM prices for its modular laptops/mainboards/desktops from climbing too high as a result of the global memory shortage , though storage hasn't fared quite so well . And now, RAM unfortunately isn't faring so well, either. The company explains in its monthly memory and storage pricing update that its memory module supplier has raised its prices substantially. And unfortunately it's to the extent that Framework isn't going to be able to eat the extra cost. "We recently received a cost update from our LPCAMM2 supplier that goes far beyond anything we had predicted and anything we’re able to absorb without placing our ability to operate at real financial risk. Rather than the low-to-mid double digit percentage increase we had forecast from Q2 to Q3 on LPCAMM2 costs, we received prices that are more than double that of the prior inventory we had brought in." Framework's explanation for what it's having to change given this is a little confusing, but essentially it looks like all remaining 64 GB module stock the company has will cover already-existing laptop and mainboard pre-orders using these modules, but…

title: The challenges of developing the colony sim, from Dungeon Keeper to Dwarf Fortress and beyond
summary: PC Gamer interviews the genre's founding fathers.
body: From the archives: This feature was originally published in PC Gamer #305 (UK, June 2017) . It appears below as originally written, with only minor changes in formatting and presentation. No gaming genre can generate stories quite like the colony sim. Almost entirely unique to PC gaming, colony sims task players with building and sustaining a habitat for a group of AI characters, which could be anything from dwarfs to demons. Although the player controls how the colony is shaped, constructing rooms or buildings and filling them with useful objects, they do not control the inhabitants directly. Instead, the colonists interact with their surroundings and other NPCs via AI systems that simulate their needs and desires. Through these desires and the player’s ability (or inability) to meet them, colony sims produce incredible, incidental plotlines; tales of dwarves who delve too greedily and too deep, unleashing hordes of monsters into their fortress, of bitter rivalries between demonic creatures that tear apart the plans of an evil dungeon master, of prisoners who explode into riot because the warden forgot to build a shower block. It’s the autonomous personalities of these AI individ…

title: Steam update makes gifting much easier, adds wishlist organization features
summary: Aunts and uncles rejoice: Sending gifts on Steam has been simplified.
body: Buying someone else a Steam game got a lot easier today. You no longer have to be Steam friends with the person to whom you're sending a gift, and don't even need a Steam account. You can also select gifts directly from a shared wishlist. Here are today's Steam gifting changes , in short: It's now possible to gift a game without having a Steam account. (Previously, guest checkout could only be used for digital gift card and hardware purchases) You also don't need to know the recipient's Steam account, and can gift games and digital gift cards with just an email address (even if it isn't the address associated with their Steam account) You can now gift games across regions. You need to know the recipient's Steam account for this, as the price you pay will be the price of the game in their region You can now select and purchase gifts directly from a shared wishlist Previously, sending a gift on Steam meant asking the recipient for their Steam account name and friending them, which dampens the surprise a bit. My only wish now is for a simple way to stuff a Steam game into a physical card for birthdays and holidays. Steam retired physical gift cards recently, citing fraud. (With these…

title: Blizzard is investigating a World of Warcraft cheating scandal after game master 'death-touched' bosses for friends without realizing it was being recorded
summary: One of the players then went on a Twitch stream to spill the beans.
body: A cheating scandal has erupted in the World of Warcraft community after a Blizzard developer was caught using special game master (GM) abilities to kill two bosses for some friends. The GM seemingly didn't realize that one of the players was recording combat logs of the whole thing. Late on Tuesday, Reddit user Acrobatic_Pianist688 posted evidence of the GM's meddling on the WoW subreddit. Their post links to a combat log that shows a non-player ability called "Area Death (TEST)" being used to instantly kill a boss with nearly 90% of its health left. Not long after, a commenter on the thread found the GM using a separate insta-kill ability on a boss earlier in the run , too. Surprisingly, one of the players from the accused group went onto a Twitch stream to talk about what happened . When streamer Yumytv asked them to explain, they said they were just short of finishing the Mythic+ dungeon in time to get a seasonal title when they called for help from their GM friend: "I go to my GM friend and I'm like bro please can you do me a solid? This thing has 20 million [health] bro, can you please just one-shot it?" The player went on to say that they don't care if Blizzard disqualifies …

title: Indie game gets last minute Steam delay while Valve reviews its 'dozens of untextured polygons depicting ass and boobs'
summary: Future? No Thanks! is the latest game to get caught up in Steam's ambiguous content guidelines.
body: Earlier this month, indie political satirist game dev Molleindustria—pseudonym of Paolo Pedercini, who created games like Democratic Socialism Simulator and New York Times Simulator —announced that he was moving up the release of his latest game. Despite having previously promised to launch the game on the same day as GTA 6 "in order to humiliate Rockstar in the open-world genre and deliver the killing blow to the Western game industry," Future? No Thanks!, would instead launch today, on July 22. Now that the day has arrived, Molleindustria's launch plans have changed once again. The developer announced a last minute delay for Future? No Thanks! on Bluesky, saying that the game has been the subject of an unexpectedly lengthy Steam review process due to the presence of nudity during one of the game's sequences. Some sad news regarding today’s release: — @molleindustria.org ( @molleindustria.org.bsky.social ) 2026-07-22T19:08:49.374Z Using a sarcastic tone characteristic of Pedercini's previous work, Molleindustria said that its launch timeline estimates "were foolish to disregard the only authority on the matter: Steam, which has kept our game under review for more than three weeks…

title: StarCraft 2 is included in Blizzard's BlizzCon digital goodies, which is more evidence a new game will be announced soon, right?
summary: Is it just me or is Blizzard gearing up to announce the rumored StarCraft shooter?
body: Not to fully transform into the Pepe Silvia meme pointing at a wall of "evidence" but I'm convinced Blizzard is gearing up to announce something StarCraft-related at BlizzCon this year. The first reliable clue came from Bloomberg reporter Jason Schreier's 2024 book on Blizzard's history where he said the studio was "incubating" a StarCraft shooter ( not that one ) under the helm of former Far Cry producer Dan Hay. A couple of years passed with no news on that StarCraft project. But in April of this year, Blizzard was looking to hire a lead designer of innovation for an "AAA open-world shooter" using Unreal Engine—a game engine that it's never used before. Blizzard game designer Jeff Hamilton said on Bluesky that the person who got the job would have a "fairly unique design role" as part of the team. While that was the last direct evidence for a new StarCraft game, Blizzard recently surprised-dropped a massive patch for StarCraft 2 , which feels like the kind of thing you'd do to remind people you still care about the series. The update wasn't just a bunch of bug fixes and minor tweaks either. Blizzard said the focus was to spice it up with meta-changing tweaks to early- and mid-ga…

title: Microsoft is bringing original Xbox exclusives to PC for the first time, starting with four 'classic games'
summary: Xbox Backward Compatibility on PC, as it's known, is now live.
body: As it continues to try smoothing the waters churned up by the recent layoff of thousands of people , Xbox has followed up last week's big Fallout infodump with the launch of a new " Backward Compatibility on PC " program that will bring old-time Xbox games, previously exclusive to the platform, to PC and compatible handhelds for the first time. The program is launching with four "classic original XBOX games," as Microsoft described them. I'm not sure I'd use that characterization myself, but to be fair I haven't played any of these games (although I did enjoy the original Crimson Skies), and PC Gaming Show maestro Jake Tucker informs me that "Conker was the shit." I am not convinced, but anyway, here's the starting lineup: BLiNX: The Time Sweeper Conker: Live and Reloaded Crimson Skies: High Road to Revenge Fuzion Frenzy All four games are now available for purchase on PC and are also offered through all Game Pass plans, and if you already own a license for them on console that will carry over to your PC as well. As well as being playable on new hardware, the PC-compatible versions will offer new features including customizable graphics options, and language and audio settings. (I…

title: Star Wars Zero Company's story 'is a linear one from beginning to end', Bit Reactor says, so don't expect branching paths or romance options
summary: "We are excited about pushing the genre forward with a more cinematic approach."
body: With Star Wars Zero Company , developer Bit Reactor aims to create a tactics RPG with characters and narrative at the forefront . It's got proper, big-budget cutscenes, you can talk to your buddies, and you can customise and upgrade your squad. However, the developers have gone on record in a new Discord Q&A to say, no, don't expect to have control over how the story unfolds. Earlier this year, our own Ted Litchfield spoke to narrative director Aaron Contreras , who explained that while Bit Reactor wants its cast to have real friction, Star Wars Zero Company is "not a personal fantasy game." Those "player-centric romance" options that seem to pop up in so many games today won't appear in Zero Company. This was reaffirmed in today's Q&A, where the developer plainly states, "No, there aren’t any romance systems or branching paths" in Zero Company. "While we are excited about pushing the genre forward with a more cinematic approach, this is still a turn-based tactics game at its heart where adept skill and strategy are needed to overcome tense combat challenges." "That said, there are story moments that can alter based on who you have in your squad, but the overall story is a linear …

title: Acer and Gigabyte will launch RTX Spark laptops too, but not before Microsoft and Co.
summary: The first batch not including Acer and Gigabyte will launch this fall.
body: Nvidia's Arm-based processor for laptops was unveiled after much waiting at Computex back in June. RTX Spark is Nvidia's first proper go at a consumer chip, combining CPU and GPU, and while it is of course targeted at AI workloads, it's also targeted at gaming. Now, Nvidia has confirmed which will be the first brands of RTX Spark laptop to come to market this fall and which will follow those. According to Nvidia , "RTX Spark systems will be available this fall from Asus, Dell Technologies, HP, Lenovo, Microsoft Surface and MSI, with models from Acer and Gigabyte to follow." The initial few aren't a big surprise given these are the exact companies that were announced as partners when RTX Spark was revealed. Of note is Acer and Gigabyte following the initial six, though. As Chinese news site the Commercial Times points out (via VideoCardz ), MSI and Asus will be the first Taiwanese companies to launch Spark lappies. But given it was the Surface Laptop Ultra that RTX Spark was most commonly shown running on at Computex, it'll be surprising if that's not the first one to market. That being said, I'm looking forward to RTX Spark laptops other than the Surface. Back when it was announce…

title: Star Wars Zero Company is going all in on Clone customisation, but my favourite armour is restricted to the Deluxe Edition
summary: "We think you’ll be very happy."
body: While it's one of the biggest games this year , new details on Star Wars Zero Company have been few and far between, even after the gameplay trailer at the start of June. That changed yesterday when developer Bit Reactor hosted a Discord Q&A answering the community's questions on everything from the game's length to permadeath. The thing that stood out to me the most, as a fanatic for character customisation, is how Zero Company is handling its Clone Troopers. User 'WhiteFeather87' kicked things off by asking what we were all wondering: Can you have a full Clone team? The answer is yes, but for two reasons. Firstly, all operators actually have access to Clone armour cosmetics from the start of the game. Secondly, you'll unlock Clone Trooper operators "after a certain point in the story", CEO and creative director Greg Foertsch explains, "allowing you to have a full team of Clone Troopers." (Image credit: Bit Reactor) The coolest detail Bit Reactor confirmed is that Clone helmets will actually add voice modulation to characters. Discord user 'HaPeanut' dug deeper, asking specifically what kind of Clone customisation options we can expect, like ARF, paratroopers, or commandoes. Whil…

title: Someone has already got a regular Nvidia graphics card working on Windows on Arm thanks to RTX Spark drivers, though gaming performance doesn't yet look ideal
summary: Still, it's a nice peek into what is surely to come.
body: For the longest time, Windows on Arm has been the purview of Snapdragon processors with Adreno CPUs (and occasionally some repurposed Mac silicon). A couple of months ago, though, Nvidia finally announced RTX Spark , an Arm processor with some real GPU grunt. And it seems that might have sparked (sorry) the onset of being able to use at least some Nvidia GPUs with the forked operating system. As spotted by Windows Latest , a developer on Chinese forum Bilibili has managed to get an RTX 4060 working on Windows on Arm using a driver based on the "RTX Spark laptop driver". The dev responsible, VoidTech , suggests this is driver 590. This is an older Nvidia driver, as we're currently at version 610.74 , and it reportedly doesn't support DGX Spark processors that RTX Spark is based on. All this is to say, I'm not sure exactly what is going on with the specific driver in question, but VoidTech does explicitly say it's based on an RTX Spark driver. I have little reason to doubt it, especially given there are now some official drivers for RTX Spark in the wild . The dev gets some games up and running, including the Black Myth: Wukong benchmark. Focusing on that as our test case for a mome…

title: The Elder Scrolls Online is already feeling the impact of Xbox's layoffs, ironically hurting the cash shop
summary: The MMO is already reeling from reduced "team bandwidth".
body: Earlier this month, Microsoft gutted The Elder Scrolls Online , with 379 staff across ZeniMax gone . And this is the second time it's happened in two years. As Harvey and I put it a few weeks ago, ESO's developers deserved better . The developer, now claiming to be the same size it was 10 years ago (similar to id Software), has just debuted ESO's new seasonal structure but will have to continue carving that path with reduced capabilities. Mere weeks after these layoffs, and we're already seeing their effects. Community manager Kevin Gbolie posted an update on the ESO forums outlining updates to the Crown Store, the game's premium cash shop. "To help manage workload, we have decided to remove the Web Crown Store from the official elderscrollsonline.com website on Wednesday, July 22," Gbolie explains. "The ability to acquire crowns via the website will remain and, of course, this does not affect the items that will be offered on the Crown Store in The Elder Scrolls Online, you will just need to login into the game to view and purchase the latest offerings." "Furthermore, for the same reason, the monthly Crown Store Showcase blog will also change." It's ironic that, to combat the inc…

title: Steam Deck sales are estimated to have plummeted by around 80% since the massive price hikes earlier this year, back of the napkin maths suggests
summary: A situation nobody wants.
body: The Steam Deck was once a budget champion, but no longer. Ever since the price hikes earlier this year, Valve's handheld has become difficult to argue for as a value proposition—and according to recent sales estimates by Boiling Steam , it appears that consumers may agree. The site has attempted to estimate the number of Steam Deck units sold by tracking its position on the Steam Top Sellers listing, and extrapolating data in relation to the entries above and below it (via XDA ). This back-of-the-napkin maths is hardly definitive, but it suggests that when the Steam Deck dropped from around fourth and fifth on the chart position to between tenth and fifteenth after the price adjustments in May of this year, the drop in weekly units sold would equate to roughly 80-82%. Boiling Steam suggests that a slip to fourteenth on the Top Sellers list from its previous top five position represents a drop in gross revenue of approximately 72%, something the outlet characterises as a "complete collapse in total sales." Presuming Boiling Steam's earlier estimate of between 11,000 and 18,000 units sold a week pre-price increase is correct, the figures would equate to between 1,400 and 3,000 units…

title: Path of Exile 2 players broke the game by summoning 2,000 skeletons and then lured unsuspecting players into the ultimate frame rate shredder
summary: A literal minion army.
body: Most players eventually lose the drive to keep grinding a new league, or season, in Path of Exile 2 and simply log off until the next one. Others gather up all their in-game money and spend it on something ridiculous, like an army of 2,000 skeletons. PoE 2 streamer Empyriangaming decided it was time to test the action RPG's limits by creating the most expensive minion builds I've ever seen. We're talking gear that cost him more in-game currency than I've earned over the course of my 700 hours in the game. There's min-maxing and then there's whatever Empyriangaming did to make six complete sets of gear to summon more minions than the game can even handle. Normally, a minion build has around 10 to 15 skeletons accompanying them in PoE 2, but Empyriangaming's crew did some math to figure out how to squeeze out as many of them as possible. The trick is to reduce the resource cost of each minion into single digits and to stack up as much of that resource as you can. Unfortunately, the game refuses to let you summon more than 255 of each minion type before deleting them all and resetting the counter back to zero, so Empyriangaming recruited some friends to bring their own armies. The gr…

title: Hasbro takes $56 million hit over multiple game cancellations, but it's still talking about how Baldur's Gate 3 is key to its future success
summary: Just a few years after going big on videogames, Hasbro is pulling an Xbox by narrowing its focus on its biggest brands and franchises.
body: Two years after boasting about how hard it was going on videogames, Hasbro has recorded a "$56 million non-cash impairment" in its second-quarter financial report , arising from the cancellation of several games in development that were expected to release "in 2028 and beyond." First things first: As PC Gamer's Joshua Wolens explained when Sony took a staggering $766 million impairment against Bungie, that $56 million figure isn't a direct loss. Rather, it's an unexpected decrease in value of an asset compared to its book value, separate from standard depreciation. Just a few years ago, inspired by the massive success of Baldur's Gate 3, Hasbro was going hard on videogames. In 2022 it had a half-dozen game studios working on different projects in its stable; two years later Dan Ayoub, head of digital product development at Hasbro-owned Wizards of the Coast, put a number on that effort, saying "we've got over $1 billion in games right now being developed," and adding that "videogames are an integral part of Hasbro's strategy going into the next 100 years." But it hasn't gone especially smoothly since then. A big-budget GI Joe game announced in 2021 is in trouble, and the deal for a…

title: A new friendslop game is already Steam's 12th-most wishlisted game a mere week after it was announced
summary: The takeoff hasn't been without some turbulence, however.
body: About once a week I head on over Steam to peek at the most wishlisted games because I want to know: what are PC gamers looking forward to most? Are there any highly anticipated games on the brink of release? And is anything new blowing up This week I definitely spotted something new: Dear Passengers, a co-op game about running "the world's worst airline" with your friends, is now seated comfortably at number 12 on Steam's top wishlists . That's impressive because the chaotic friendslop game was only announced a week ago. Here's the trailer if you missed it: "You and your friends are the crew," says developer Flexus. "Your job is to deliver passengers and cargo to their destination. Preferably in one piece. While one of you pilots the plane, the others keep the cabin together, serve the passengers, and deal with whatever goes wrong before it gets much worse." And things definitely look like they get worse. Turbulence, bad weather, bird strikes, and dangerous cargo can lead to all sorts of disasters while in flight, and of course there's no greater threat than your friends who might open the doors at altitude and get everyone sucked out of the plane. It looks like chaotic fun, and t…

title: Palworld players have harnessed the power of selective breeding to create 'infinite bomb' Pals that can blow themselves up, repeatedly, forever
summary: Is that good?
body: Palworld, as our breeding guide shows, lets you accomplish some wild things by playing matchmaker for your creatures. You can gain early access to late-game Pals, spawn variant subspecies, and—as players have recently discovered—breed a genetic monstrosity that can repeatedly blow itself up in combat without taking any damage in the process. This exciting advancement in living munitions development was uncovered by Japanese X user @gn02759909, who demonstrated that they "accidentally made a super crazy infinite bomb" by uploading a video of their Pal casually sprinting at an enemy boss, one-shotting it by self-detonating, and then emerging unscathed from the smoke plume. 超やばい無限爆弾を作ってしまいました😱#パルワールド #Palworld pic.twitter.com/ldK0igXGxh July 20, 2026 It's not unusual to see Tocotoco, the colorful bird Pal used in @gn02759909, exploding itself: By default, any Tocotoco a player catches comes with the Implode skill—something most players will become intimately familiar with after their base suffers its first invasion of a Tocotoco Implode Unit—and will eventually learn an upgraded form, Megaton Implode. Using Implode, a Pal "risks its life to cause a violent explosion," dealing a heav…

title: Splitgate studio 1047 Games is laying off more people just a month after releasing its latest game
summary: Multiple employees say they've been let go in a new round of cuts.
body: A little less than a month after the release of its Titanfall-inspired shooter Empulse, developer 1047 Games has reportedly laid off more employees. While the studio hasn't made a formal announcement of the cuts, multiple employees on LinkedIn (via Game Developer ) have said they've been let go in what one described as "the third wave of studio layoffs." Reports of layoffs at 1047 began to surface last week. "A bunch of us, 'the crew' aboard the 1047 train, are getting off on this post-Empulse station," QA director Igor Krinitskiy wrote at the time. "It has been an interesting ride, bumpy at times, narrowly avoiding derailment and with everyone working hard to keep that 1047 train going." More recent messages also point to multiple cuts across the studio. UI engineer Iannis Stefan Palaczkos wrote that "the studio has been sadly hit by another wave of layoffs , and I got caught in that wave," while concept artist Jonathan Kuo said separately that "as of the end of this week, it will be my last day at 1047 Games due to the third wave of studio layoffs ." 1047 Games found fast success several years ago with Splitgate: Arena Warfare, a 'Halo with portals' mashup that was an immediate …

title: Jimothy, a particularly round raccoon and the internet's latest sensation, has now broken into games
summary: Fortnite crossover when?
body: Yesterday I learnt about Jimothy, an adorably round raccoon (possibly with a spine condition) scuttling around Seattle that's gone viral thanks to his unique appearance. It was only a matter of time then before all the brand accounts joined in on the raccoon's newfound celebrity status. He's already crossing over with a bunch of your favourite games. I first saw World of Warcraft share a picture of what lil' Jimothy would look like in the MMO and, frankly, I'd very much like him as a pet in-game. RuneScape followed up with a very similar render of Jimothy in the old school style, and Dead by Daylight gave him glowing eyes and chest, and bloody tentacles for some reason. Jimothy ❤️ https://t.co/MKhyfCm2mq pic.twitter.com/2LtV3E2eqX July 18, 2026 Mouse: P.I. For Hire tastefully drew Jimothy in its 1930s rubber hose, film noir style. Jack Pepper could have a new sidekick, and he probably likes cheese too—raccoons don't tend to be picky. Jimothy has been spotted in Mouseburg 🐭 pic.twitter.com/LYcA8QYR5H July 21, 2026 And then, at the mercy of social media's algorithms, I unfortunately saw The Sims chime in with a rather terrifying depiction of Jimothy. I'm convinced that's not an ani…

title: A modder has brought No Man's Sky-style seamless takeoffs to Starfield, and even in beta they look pretty great
summary: Going up.
body: One of Starfield's big problems is that its core fantasy runs contrary to the tech it's built on. Bethesda's space RPG is meant to evoke the majesty and scale of the cosmos. It's about exploration, curiosity, and wonder. And yet, it exists in an engine that necessitates a whole lot of loading screens, and so the final frontier feels like a series of small rooms. In particular, the game's takeoff and landing sequences drew criticism for shoving in loading screens where games like No Man's Sky had seamless transitions. Well, no more! Sort of! A modder named 0xBobby is hard at work to rid Starfield's takeoff sequences of the loading screen scourge. Seamless Planet Takeoffs is a mod for Starfield that makes departing its planets an at least somewhat immersive experience. Rather than cutting to a third-person view of your ship as it jets away, you remain in the cockpit, watching the planet's atmosphere give way to the void as you ascend. It's not perfect, which is probably why it's in beta. It's not removing the loads so much as it is masking them, which means that you can still kind of identify the point at which the game shunts you from one cell to another. 0xBobby writes that "the s…

title: Arctic Freezer 36-S review
summary: Surprisingly effective for the money.
body: The Arctic Freezer 36-S lands at the right time. Prices are rising for PC builds but you can claw some money back with this cooler. It costs just $22 / £19 . A small price to pay for ample cooling for your processor. And it is, genuinely, good. Arctic has plenty of accolades to its name when it comes to cooling. One of its more popular designs, the Arctic Freezer 36 , is our pick for the best budget air cooler . With two fans, an effective heatsink design, and a great price tag, it's all anyone needs to chill most of today's popular gaming chips. But could you make do with less? That's what the Freezer 36-S puts to the test. A few cuts have been made to the Freezer 36-S compared to the Freezer 36, including trimming down to just one fan. But it still has a lot going for it, especially when it comes to performance, as you can see in the charts below . The Freezer 36-S performs really well considering its size and price tag. Though price is a sticking point: the Freezer 36-S is going up against the best and when you can get the Freezer 36 for very nearly the same cost, it's tough to argue for the slightly cheaper model. Buy if... ✅ You've spent all your budget elsewhere: So you've b…

title: Developer of Burnout spiritual successor Wreckreation has formed a new studio working on a sequel
summary: "We've reached a point where it makes sense for the project to have a new home, one that we own completely."
body: To this day, the now-stagnant Burnout series is still the prime example of an arcade racer that every other contemporary is compared to. Naturally, it's spurred on a bunch of imitators and spiritual successors over the years, especially after the decline of Criterion Games into a support studio at EA. Nowadays, instead of pioneering racing games, it's a "Battlefield Studio" . One such spiritual successor was 2025's Wreckreation, created by Three Fields Entertainment, a small team of veterans, including from Criterion Games, making games since 1999. Unfortunately, Wreckreaction launched to mixed reviews, not quite reaching the heights of its inspiration. But it's not the end of the team or the series. "We've made a double announcement today," the developer's latest statement on Patreon reads. "We're making a sequel to Wreckreation … It's built around aggression, pressure, risk, and of course consequence." "Over the last six months we've been talking directly to players about what they want from a modern arcade driving game, and Wreckreation 2 is the result of those conversations," said studio head Fiona Sperry. "From handling and progression to events and world design, our communit…

title: The Witcher 3's expansion reveal is scheduled for next month
summary: And other headlines that could come from 2026 or 2015.
body: CD Projekt Red sent us all scurrying for our calendars when it announced, last May, that 2015's The Witcher 3 would be getting a brand-new expansion in the year of our lord 2027. It's called Songs of the Past, it's set to be "a little bit closer to Blood and Wine" in terms of size, and it's being co-developed alongside Fool's Theory, the studio that's also remaking the first Witcher. And that, for now, is pretty much all we know. But take heart, because 'knowing perhaps a small amount more' is just around the corner. In a post on X , CDPR announced that Songs of the Past would show its face as part of Gamescom's Opening Night Live event—the secret third Geoff Keighley annual thing which I forget about every year—on August 25. Our Path to #gamescom 2026 starts with #OpeningNightLive. On August 25, catch a glimpse of The Witcher 3: Wild Hunt - Songs of the Past during the show!🗓️ Mark your calendars: watch ONL on August 25 at 11am PT / 2pm ET / 8pm CEST pic.twitter.com/6nCKo9ETbk July 21, 2026 I think I might be more intrigued by this than any other upcoming release, which is either a damning indictment of the release calendar, or a hearty testament to CDPR's games (it is both, I t…
